action_separation.description: Controller diverges from max-pressure on only 2.82% of decisions, far below the 5% action-separation gate. The controller is effectively a near-clone of pressure in most states, with the completion-critical regime activating uniformly but failing to produce differentiated actions.

regime_collapse.description: Regime classifier collapsed entirely to completion_critical: all 270 controller-row observations land in a single regime. Slack, storage_binding, and cascade_risk regimes were never activated despite the training split including spillback stress and downstream blockage scenarios designed to trigger them. The classifier lacks discriminative power.

baseline_regret.description: Minimax regret is ~1.95M against switching_loss_max_pressure. All baselines except capacity_aware_pressure and occupancy_capacity_aware_pressure show six-figure or higher mean regret. The controller is consistently outperformed, especially by switching-aware and delay-aware baselines that model mechanisms v1.7 ignores.

safety_harms.description: 243 safety-guard violations across all 6 baselines. Violations span total_delay (88), unfinished_vehicle_count (133), and penalized_avg_travel_time (22). Worst offender is switching_loss_max_pressure with 92 violations. Even max_pressure produces 23 violations. The controller causes real harm: more vehicles stuck unfinished and higher delay than baselines.

root_cause_ranking: 1. Regime classifier collapse: the four-regime classifier (slack/storage_binding/cascade_risk/completion_critical) collapsed to a single regime (completion_critical=100%). This is the root failure -- without regime discrimination, the controller cannot apply regime-appropriate corrections and falls back to near-pressure behavior everywhere.

2. Safety envelope not regime-dependent: with all observations classified as completion_critical, the safety envelope is applied uniformly and is too conservative in states that should be slack, while being too loose in states that should be cascade_risk or storage_binding. This produces 243 safety-guard violations across all baselines.

3. No switching/coordination mechanism: the controller lacks any explicit switching-loss or inter-intersection coordination model. switching_loss_max_pressure is the worst baseline (regret ~1.95M), confirming that switching cost awareness is critical for arterial networks with coordinated signal timing.

4. Uncalibrated rollout predictions: the 3-step rollout with H_rollout=3.0 produces advantage-gate activations (95.9% rate) that are disconnected from actual performance improvements. The gate activates pervasively but the resulting actions barely differ from pressure (2.82% change rate), indicating the rollout model does not identify genuinely beneficial departures.

v18_implications.regime_classifier: Must be first gate -- regime balance must pass before training. v1.8 should validate regime distribution on held-out states before any closed-loop run. Require minimum 10% activation in at least 3 of 4 regimes.
v18_implications.safety_envelope: Must be regime-dependent. Different regimes need different safety thresholds. The current flat tolerance model is structurally insufficient.
v18_implications.switching: Must be first-class mechanism. Switching loss appears in the primary endpoint and in the worst baseline. v1.8 must include explicit switching cost in the controller objective or dual decomposition.
v18_implications.rollout: Must pass sign-accuracy gate. The rollout model should be validated for directional accuracy (does the predicted improvement match the actual improvement?) before being used for action selection.

training_iterations_summary: Single-parameter controller (finite_storage_completion_primal_dual_v1_7) evaluated on 378 training rows (54 controller rows x 6 baselines x 6 seeds x 3 demand multipliers). Row audit is clean (378/378 completed, 0 missing/failed/duplicate). Mechanism diagnostics show total failure: regime collapse to single class, action separation at 2.82% vs 5% target, minimax regret ~1.95M against switching_loss_max_pressure, and 243 safety-guard violations. Decision: REJECT. Claim scope remains non-confirmatory -- training rows are not claim evidence per protocol.
